title: Rustic Wooden Candle Holders
summary: Solid 4x4 posts trimmed with mitered lattice and moulding at top and bottom — a fast, satisfying small build in a set of graduated heights.
description:
A single 4x4 post is the whole holder — the craft is entirely in the mitered trim ring at the base and the smaller ring near the top, both cut so the four corners meet cleanly. Building a matched set at a few different heights, as the source does (3.5", 4.5", and 5"), reads far better as a grouped display than any one holder alone would.

The top trim is deliberately set with a 1/4" reveal below the post's top edge rather than flush, which keeps the trim from looking like an afterthought cap and instead reads as an intentional design line.

tools.note: A compound miter saw cuts both the post to length and the mitered trim rings.

materials.name: Pine or cedar, 4x4, scrap
materials.note: Cut to your desired holder height(s).

cutList.note: Cut to desired height — 3.5", 4.5", and 5" shown as a graduated set.
Long point to long point, both ends 45° miter, ends not parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends 45° miter, ends not parallel.

steps.title: Cut the post
steps.body: Cut the 4x4 to your desired length with a compound miter saw.

steps.title: Add the bottom trim
steps.body: Miter the bottom trim pieces around the base of the post and attach with glue and 1" brad or pin nails.

steps.title: Add the top trim
steps.body: Repeat with the smaller top trim pieces, but leave a 1/4" reveal below the top of the post rather than mounting it flush.

images.alt: Set of rustic wooden candle holders with mitered trim
